key_file_excerpts.composer.json:
{
  "name": "smartresponsor/accessing",
  "description": "Symfony 8 account access application for registration, verification, MFA, recovery, sessions, and security events.",
  "type": "project",
  "license": "proprietary",
  "require": {
    "php": "^8.4",
    "ext-ctype": "*",
    "ext-iconv": "*",
    "doctrine/doctrine-bundle": "3.1",
    "doctrine/doctrine-migrations-bundle": "3.6",
    "doctrine/orm": "^3.3",
    "scheb/2fa-bundle": "8.5",
    "scheb/2fa-totp": "8.5",
    "symfony/console": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/dotenv": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/form": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/framework-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/mailer": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/monolog-bundle": "^3.10",
    "symfony/notifier": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/password-hasher": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/rate-limiter": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/runtime": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/security-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/security-csrf": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/serializer": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/twig-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/translation": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/validator": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/yaml": "8.0.*",
    "symfonycasts/reset-password-bundle": "^1.23",
    "symfonycasts/verify-email-bundle": "^1.17",
    "twig/extra-bundle": "^3.15",
    "twig/twig": "^3.15"
  },
  "require-dev": {
    "doctrine/doctrine-fixtures-bundle": "^4.1",
    "fakerphp/faker": "^1.24",
    "friendsofphp/php-cs-fixer": "3.94",
    "phpmd/phpmd": "^2.15",
    "phpstan/phpstan": "^2.1",
    "symfony/browser-kit": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/css-selector": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/maker-bundle": "^1.64",
    "symfony/panther": "^2.2",
    "symfony/phpunit-bridge": "^7.3"
  },
  "autoload": {
    "psr-4": {
      "App\\": "src/"
    }
  },
  "autoload-dev": {
    "psr-4": {
      "App\\Tests\\": "tests/"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "php -l $(git ls-files '*.php')",
    "cs:check": "php-cs-fixer fix --dry-run --diff --verbose",
    "cs:fix": "php-cs-fixer fix --verbose",
    "stan": "phpstan analyse",
    "test": "vendor/bin/simple-phpunit",
    "pipeline:local:full": [
      "@lint",
      "@cs:check",
      "@stan",
      "@test"
    ]
  },
  "extra": {
    "public-dir": "public",
    "product-name": "Accessing",
    "core-entity": "Account"
  },
  "minimum-stability": "stable",
  "prefer-stable": true,
  "config": {
    "allow-plugins": {
      "symfony/runtime": true
    }
  }
}

verdict.summary: Strong Symfony 8 account-access service with clear manifests and broad tests; main gap is delivery/ops automation and the missing Antora report path.
strengths: Clear product identity and scope documented (manifests, README).
Symfony 8 stack with security, 2FA, verification, and reset-password bundles.
Well-structured domain folders (Entity, Repository, Service, ValueObject, Interfaces).
Comprehensive test layout (Unit, Integration, Functional, Panther, Playwright).
Static analysis and code style tooling wired via composer scripts.
Recent migration history suggesting active development.
risks: No GitHub Actions workflows detected; CI/CD automation is missing.
Antora report path from registry is absent in the repo, blocking report rendering.
Dist-only configs for phpunit/phpstan may lead to inconsistent local setups/enforcement.
Limited observability beyond logging bundles; no metrics/tracing in evidence.
Proprietary license may constrain external adoption.
next_actions: Add a minimal GitHub Actions workflow running composer install and scripts pipeline:local:full.
Promote phpunit.xml.dist to phpunit.xml with coverage thresholds and run in CI.
Promote phpstan.neon.dist to phpstan.neon with defined level/baseline and gate in CI.
Add Playwright E2E job to CI (npm ci; playwright install; npm run test:e2e).
Create the Antora report at .antora-src/modules/ROOT/pages/component/accessing/report/index.adoc to unblock publishing.
Introduce basic metrics/tracing instrumentation and dashboards, beyond logging.
Document and pin operational runbook details in config/MANIFEST.md or README (envs, secrets, migrations).
notes: Assessment grounded in repository scan at master bb0013dfbd4df3f201d4cae167f5de4bdf58967f on 2026-04-05.
Composer requires PHP ^8.4 and Symfony 8; security and rate-limiting bundles are present.
Multiple *MANIFEST.md documents provide intentional architecture/product context.

product.summary: Product identity and scope are explicitly documented and aligned with an account-access service (registration, verification, MFA, recovery, sessions, security events).
risks: Antora report path is missing, reducing discoverability of product documentation.
improvements: Publish the component report at the configured Antora path and cross-link from README.

architecture.summary: Symfony 8 architecture with clear domain separation (entities, repositories, services, value objects) and intent captured in manifests.
risks: Architectural governance and boundaries are described but not yet enforced via CI or automated checks.
improvements: Encode key architecture rules in static analysis/phpmd rulesets and enforce in CI.

runtime.summary: Runtime shape is standard Symfony (public front controller, configuration bootstrap, routes, env files) with logging and rate limiting available.
risks: Operational runtime characteristics (healthchecks, readiness probes, instrumentation) are not visible in the repo.
improvements: Add health/readiness endpoints and basic runtime instrumentation to support operations.

qa.summary: Broad test coverage surface (Unit, Integration, Functional, browser/E2E) with static analysis and style tools available via composer scripts.
risks: CI gating for tests and static analysis is absent; phpunit/phpstan only have dist configs, risking drift across environments.
improvements: Create phpunit.xml with coverage thresholds; add phpstan.neon with level/baseline; run both in CI with fail-on-violation.

ops.summary: Basic operational readiness exists locally (logging via Monolog, composer pipeline script), but delivery automation and observability depth are missing.
risks: No GitHub Actions workflows found; no visible metrics/tracing/alerting setup.
improvements: Add CI workflows running the full composer pipeline and Playwright tests; add metrics/tracing and basic alerting.

market.summary: Business-facing positioning is clear for an account-access component; repository manifests and README communicate scope and core entity.
risks: Proprietary license may limit community adoption and contributions.
improvements: Clarify intended distribution and integration model in README (targets, SLAs, versioning policy).

overall.summary: Pre-RC posture with strong product/architecture and testing surface; primary blocker is the absence of CI/CD and missing Antora report path.
risks: Lack of CI/CD reduces release confidence; report path absence blocks portfolio visibility.
improvements: Introduce minimal CI/CD and publish the Antora report to elevate to RC readiness.
